author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XVIII. 15.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “If thy brother shall trespassagainst thee, go and tell him his faultbetween thee and him alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, since He had used vehement language against them that cause offense, and on every hand had moved them to fear; in order that the offended might not in this way on the other hand become supine, neither supposing all to be cast upon others, should be led on to another vice, soften in themselves, and desiring to be humored in everything, and run upon the shoal of pride; seest thou how He again checks them also, and commands the telling of the faults to be between the two alone, lest by the testimony of the many he should render his accusation heavier, and the other, become excited to opposition, should continue incorrigible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “Between thee and him alone,” and, “If he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is, “If he shall hear thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If he shall condemn himself, if he shall be persuaded that he has done wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou hast gained thy brother.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not say, Thou hast a sufficient revenge, but, “Thou hast gained thy brother,” to show that there is a common loss from the enmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “He hath gained himself only,” but, “thou too hast gained him,” whereby He showed that both the one and the other were losers before this, the one of his brother, the other of his own salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This, when He sat on the mount also, He advised; at one time bringing him who has given the pain to him that had been pained, and saying, “Be reconciled to thy brother,”and at another commanding him that had been wronged to forgive his neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He taught men to say, “Forgive us our debts, like as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here He is devising another mode.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not him that gave the pain, doth He now call upon,but him that was pained He brings to this one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because this who hath done the wrong would not easily come to make excuse, out of shame, and confusion of face, He draws that other to him, and not merely so, but in such way as also to correct what hath been done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He saith not, “Accuse,” nor “Charge him,” nor “Demand satisfaction, and an account,” but, “Tell him of his fault,”saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he is held in a kind of stupor through anger and shame with which he is intoxicated; and thou, who art in health, must go thy way to him that is ill, and make the tribunal private, and the remedy such as may be readily received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to say, “Tell him of his fault,” is nothing else than “Remind him of his error,” tell him what thou hast suffered at his hand, which very thing, if it be done as it ought, is the part of one making excuse for him, and drawing him over earnestly to a reconciliation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, if he should disobey, and be disposed to abide in hardness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Take with thyself yet one or two, that in the mouth of two witnesses every word may be established.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more he is shameless, and bold, the more ought we to be active for his cure, not in anger and indignation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the physician in like manner, when he sees the malady obstinate, doth not give up nor grow impatient, but then makes the more preparation; which He commands us to do in this case too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since thou appearedst to be too weak alone, make thyself more powerful by this addition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely the two are sufficient to convict him that hath sinned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He seeketh not the good of him that hath been pained only, but of him also that hath given the pain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the person injured is this one who is taken captive by his passion, he it is that is diseased, and weak, and infirm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He often sends the other to this one, now alone, and now with others; but if he continue in it, even with the church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Tell it,” saith He, “to the Church.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He were seeking this one’s advantage only, He would not have commanded to pardon, seventy times seven, one repenting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He would not so often have set so many over him to correct his passion; but if he had remained incorrigible after the first conference would have let him be; but now once, and twice, and thrice, He commands to attempt his cure, and now alone and now with two, now with more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, with respect to them that are without He saith no such thing, but, “If any one smite thee,” He saith, “on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also,”but here not in such wise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what Paul meaneth, saying, “What have I to do to judge them also that are without?”but the brethren he commands both to tell of their faults, and to avoid them, and to cut them off, not being obedient, that they may be ashamed; this Himself also doeth here, making these laws about the brethren; and He sets threeover him for teachers and judges, to teach him the things that are done at the time of his drunkenness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though it be himself that hath said and done all those unreasonable things, yet he will need others to teach him this, like as the drunken man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For anger and sin is a more frantic thingthan any drunkenness, and puts the soul in greater distraction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who, for instance, was wiser than David?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet for all that, when he had sinned he perceived it not, his lust keeping in subjection all his reasoning powers, and like some smoke filling his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore he stood in need of a lantern from the prophet, and of words calling to his mind what he had done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore here also He brings these to him that hath sinned, to reason with him about the things he had done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for what reason doth He command this one to tell him of his fault, and not another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this man he would endure more quietly, this, who hath been wronged, who hath been pained, who hath been despitefully used.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one doth not bear in the same way being told by another of one’s fault concerning him that hath been insulted, as by the insulted person himself, especially when this person is alone convicting him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when he who should demand justice against him, even this one appears to be caring for his salvation, this will have more power than anything in the world to shame him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how this is done not for the sake of just punishment, but of amendment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He doth not at once command to take with him the two, but when himself hath failed; and not even then doth He send forth a multitude against him; but makes the addition no further than two, or even one; but when he has contemned these too, then and not till then He brings him out to the church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So much earnestness doth He show, that our neighbor’s sins be not exposed by us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed He might have commanded this from the first, but that this might not be, He did not command it, but after a first and second admonition He appoints this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is, “In the mouth of two or three witnesses every word shall be established?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou hast a sufficient testimony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His meaning is, that thou hast done all thy part, that thou hast left undone none of the things which it pertained to thee to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But if he shall neglect to hear them also, tell it to the church,” that is, to the rulers of it; “but if he neglect to hear the church, let him be to thee as an heathen man and a publican.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after this such a one is incurably diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, how everywhere He putteth the publican for an example of the greatest wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For above too He saith, “Do not even the publicans the same?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And further on again, “Even the publicans and the harlots shall go before you into the Kingdom of Heaven,”that is, they who are utterly reprobated and condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let them hearken, who are rushing upon unjust gains, who are counting up usuries upon usuries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why did He set him with these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To soothe the person wronged, and to alarm him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is this only then the punishment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, but hear also what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He did not say to the ruler of the church, “Bind such a man,” but, “If thou bind,” committing the whole matter to the person himself, who is aggrieved, and the bonds abide indissoluble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore he will suffer the utmost ills; but not he who hath brought him to account is to blame, but he who hath not been willing to be persuaded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath bound him down with twofold constraint, both by the vengeance here, and by the punishment hereafter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things hath He threatened, that these circumstances may not arise, but that fearing, at once the being cast out of the church, and the danger from the bond, and the being bound in Heaven, he may become more gentle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And knowing these things, if not at the beginning, at any rate in the multitude of the tribunals he will put off his anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, I tell you, He hath set over him a first, and a second, and a third court,so that though he should neglect to hear the first, he may yield to the second; and even if he should reject that, he may fear the third; and though he should make no account of this, he may be dismayed at the vengeance to come, and at the sentence and judgment to proceed from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And again I say unto you, that if two of you shall agree on earth as touching anything that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how by another motive also He puts down our enmities, and takes away our petty dissensions,and draws us one to another, and this not from the punishment only which hath been mentioned, but also from the good things which spring from charity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For having denounced those threats against contentiousness, He putteth here the great rewards of concord, if at least they who are of one accord do even prevail with the Father, as touching the things they ask, and have Christ in the midst of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Are there then indeed nowhere two of one accord?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, in many places, perchance even everywhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “How then do they not obtain all things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because many are causes of their failing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For either they often ask things inexpedient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why marvellest thou, if this is the case with some others, whereas it was so even with Paul, when he heard, “My grace is sufficient for thee; for my strength is perfected in weakness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or they are unworthy to be reckoned with them that heard these words, and contribute not their own part, but He seeks for such as are like them; therefore He saith “of you,” of the virtuous, of them that show forth an angelic rule of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or they pray against them that have aggrieved them, seeking for redress and vengeance; and this kind of thing is forbidden, for, “Pray,” saith He, “for your enemies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or having sins unrepented they ask mercy, which thing it is impossible to receive, not only if themselves ask it, but although others having much confidence towards God entreat for them, like as even Jeremiah praying for the Jews did hear, “Pray not thou for this people, because I will not hear thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if all things are there, and thou ask things expedient, and contribute all thine own part, and exhibit an apostolical life, and have concord and love towards thy neighbor, thou wilt obtain on thy entreaty; for the Lord is loving towards man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then because He had said, “Of my Father,” in order that He might show that it is Himself that giveth, and not He who begat Him only, He added, “For wheresoever two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? are there not two or three gathered together in His name?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There are indeed, but rarely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not merely of the assembling doth He speak, neither this doth He require only; but most surely, as I said before also, the rest of virtue too together with this, and besides, even this itself He requires with great strictness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what He saith is like this, “If any holds me the principal ground of his love to his neighbors, I will be with Him, if he be a virtuous man in other respects.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now we see the more part having other motives of friendship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For one loves, because he is loved, another because he hath been honored, a third because such a one has been useful to him in some other worldly matter, a fourth for some other like cause; but for Christ’s sake it is a difficult thing to find any one loving his neighbor sincerely, and as he ought to love him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more part are bound one to another by their worldly affairs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Paul did not love thus, but for Christ’s sake; wherefore even when not loved in such wise as he loved, he did not cease his love, because he had planted a strong root of his affection; but not so our present state, but on inquiry we shall find with most men anything likely to produce friendship rather than this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if any one bestowed on me power in so great a multitude to make this inquiry, I would show the more part bound one to another by worldly motives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is evident from the causes that work enmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because they are bound one to another by these temporalmotives, therefore they are neither fervent towards one another, nor constant, but insult, and loss of money, and envy, and love of vainglory, and every such thing coming upon them, severs the love-tie.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it finds not the root spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if indeed it were such, no worldly thing would dissolve things spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For love for Christ’s sake is firm, and not to be broken, and impregnable, and nothing can tear it asunder; not calumnies, not dangers, not death, no other thing of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though he suffer ten thousand things, who thus loves; looking to the ground of his love, he will not desist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he who loves because of being loved, should he meet with anything painful, puts an end to his love; but he who is bound by this, will never desist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also said, “Charity never faileth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what hast thou to say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That when honored he insults? that receiving benefits he was minded to slay thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even this works upon thee to love more, if thou lovest for Christ’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what things are in the rest subversive of love, these here become apt to produce it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First, because such a one is to thee a cause of rewards; secondly, because he that is so disposed stands in need of more succor, and much attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore I say, he who thus loves inquires not about race, nor country, nor wealth, nor his love to himself, nor any other such matter, but though he be hated, though he be insulted, though he be slain, continues to love, having as a sufficient ground for love, Christ; wherefore also he stands steadfast, firm, not to be overthrown, looking unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Christ too so loved his enemies, having loved the obstinate, the injurious, the blasphemers, them that hated Him, them that would not so much as see Him; them that were preferring wood and stones to Him, and with the highest love beyond which one cannot find another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For greater love hath no man than this,” He saith, “that one lay down his life for his friends.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And those even that crucified Him, and acted in so many instances with contumely against Him, see how He continues to treat with kindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even to His Father He speaks for them, saying, “Forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He sent His disciples moreover, after these things, unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This love then let us also imitate, unto this let us look, that being followers of Christ, we may attain both unto the good things here, and unto those to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might world without end.
